name: Fried Chicken with Cream Gravy
recipeInstructions:
1. COMBINE 1/2 cup milk and 1 egg in a medium bowl.
2. Combine 1 cup all-purpose flour, 2 teaspoons garlic salt, 1 tablespoon paprika, 1 tablespoon black pepper, and 1/4 teaspoon poultry seasoning in a paper or plastic bag.
3. Add a few pieces of chicken at a time, shaking to coat each piece.
4. Dip the chicken in the milk-egg mixture, then shake again in the flour mixture.
5. Reserve the remaining flour for later use.
6. Heat 1/2 to 1 inch of shortening or oil to 365°F in an electric skillet or on medium-high in a large heavy skillet.
7. Brown the chicken on all sides.
8. Reduce the heat to low (275°F) and continue cooking until the chicken is tender, about 30 to 40 minutes. Turn the chicken several times during cooking and drain on paper towels in a single layer.
9. To make the gravy, pour off all but 2 tablespoons of the fat from the skillet.
10. Stir in 2 tablespoons of the reserved seasoned flour and stir constantly over low heat for 2 minutes.
11. Whisk in the chicken stock, stirring to scrape up the brown bits on the bottom of the pan.
12. Then stir in the milk and bring to a boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
13. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
14. Serve immediately, passing the gravy separately.
